rationale: This page contains fundamental definitions and classifications of Artificial Intelligence (AI), including its stages (ANI, AGI, ASI) and related concepts like Machine Learning, NLP, LLMs, and Agentic AI. These are core technical concepts suitable for exam questions.
cleanedText:
2. IT & COMPUTER
2.1. ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
Artificial Intelligence & Related Concepts
AI: It is a technology that enables computers and machines to simulate human learning, comprehension, problem solving, decision-making.
Stages of AI
- Artificial Narrow Intelligence (ANI): Stage One: Machines imitate human behaviour, specializing in one area to solve a problem.
- Artificial General Intelligence (AGI): Stage Two: Machines can continuously learn and are as smart as humans.
- Artificial Super Intelligence (ASI): Stage Three: It is superintelligent by being self-aware and intelligent enough to surpass the cognitive abilities of humans.
Machine Learning (ML): Enables AI to imitate the way that humans learn, gradually improving its accuracy.
- Supervised learning: It uses labeled datasets to train algorithms to predict outcomes and recognize patterns.
- Unsupervised learning: It uses algorithms to analyze and cluster unlabeled data sets.
Natural language processing (NLP): Machine learning technology that enables computers to interpret and comprehend human language.
Neural Networks: Computational models that are influenced by the human brain's neural structure.
Deep learning: It is a type of machine learning that uses artificial neural networks to learn from data, similar to the way we learn.
Large Language Models (LLMs): An AI system that can understand and generate text, built on a neural network known as the transformer architecture.
- Multimodal LLM: These are LLM trained on large datasets including both text and non-textual data (image, audio, video, etc.)
Generative Pre-trained Transformer (GPT): refers to a family of LLM that can understand and generate text in natural language.
- Generative: A technology capable of producing content, such as text and imagery.
- Pre-trained: Pre-trained models are saved networks that have already been taught, using a large data set, to resolve a problem or accomplish a specific task.
- Transformer: A deep learning architecture that transforms an input into another type of output.
Agentic AI: AI systems that can autonomously make decisions, take actions and adapt to changing environments. E.g., India's first agentic AI Kruti.
